title: TaskAssignment
description: Output of L2 Task Orchestrator, input to an L4 execution agent. One task = one branch = one PR. The execution agent must complete (or fail loudly) without re-decomposing.

parent_task_id.description: Optional parent in the task DAG. NULL = top-level task.

agent_role.description: One of: code_agent, schema_agent, content_agent, pedagogy_agent, ux_agent, i18n_agent, devops_agent. Owned by /agents/README.md §4.

objective.description: What the agent is being asked to accomplish. Concrete enough that two engineers would build the same thing.

definition_of_done.description: Bullet checklist the agent must satisfy before opening a PR.

allowed_paths.description: Glob list the agent may edit. Outside this list is a hard fail.

forbidden_paths.description: Glob list the agent MUST NOT touch even if it appears 'related'. Examples: supabase/migrations/ for non-schema agents, /agents/prompts/ for everyone except evolution_agent.

context_scopes.description: Which Context Manager scopes the agent may pull from.

model_hint.description: Suggested model tier. Execution runtime may downgrade for cost; never upgrade without L2 re-approval.

max_tokens.description: Per-task token budget. Hard stop, not advisory.

evaluators_required.description: Names of evaluators that MUST run before L6 can review (e.g. unit_tests, tenant_isolation, learning_eval).
